exercises.title: Trace simple stream pipeline
Stream collect to list
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to collect long strings (> 4 chars) into a list.

blanks.hint: Method to start a stream from a collection
Collector that gathers into a List

exercises.title: Stream count and anyMatch
exercises.scenario: Terminal operations that return scalar values.
exercises.instructions:
Use stream operations to answer two questions about a list of scores.

Scores: [72, 88, 95, 65, 78, 91]

Expected output:
Above 80: 3
Any perfect 100: false
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Stream count and anyMatch
hints: anyMatch() returns boolean — true if ANY element matches

exercises.title: Stream map to extract field
exercises.scenario: Transform objects to one of their fields.
exercises.instructions:
Extract the names of all employees earning over ₹80000 as a sorted List<String>.

Employees: Aisha(95000), Raj(72000), Priya(88000), Vikram(68000), Anita(92000)

Expected output:
[Aisha, Anita, Priya]

exercises.title: distinct and limit
exercises.scenario: Remove duplicates and take first N.
exercises.instructions:
From a list with duplicates, collect the first 3 unique elements (in original order).

Input: [5, 3, 5, 1, 3, 8, 2, 1]

Expected output:
[5, 3, 1]

exercises.title: mapToDouble + average
exercises.instructions:
Compute the average product price using mapToDouble.

Products: ₹799, ₹1999, ₹649, ₹499, ₹2999

Expected output:
Average: ₹1389.00

exercises.title: Stream joining with collector
exercises.scenario: Collect stream results as a formatted string.
exercises.instructions:
Get names of all students with grade A, joined by " | ".

Students: Aisha(A), Raj(B), Priya(A), Vikram(B), Anita(A)

Expected output:
Grade A students: Aisha | Priya | Anita

test_cases.expected_stdout: Grade A students: Aisha | Priya | Anita

exercises.title: findFirst with Optional
exercises.scenario: Find first matching element, handle Optional safely.
exercises.instructions:
Find the first product priced under ₹600 that is in stock. Print it or 'None found' if nothing qualifies.

Products: Mouse(₹799,in-stock), USB Hub(₹499,in-stock), Keyboard(₹1999,in-stock), Book(₹549,out-of-stock)

Expected output:
First cheap in-stock: USB Hub
hints: found.map(p -> p.name) transforms Optional<Product> to Optional<String>

exercises.title: allMatch, anyMatch, noneMatch
exercises.instructions:
Given a list of exam scores [85, 92, 78, 95, 88], answer three questions using stream matching.

Expected output:
All passed (>=60): true
Any perfect (100): false
None failed (<60): true

test_cases.expected_stdout:
All passed (>=60): true
Any perfect (100): false
None failed (<60): true

hints: anyMatch: at least ONE must satisfy

exercises.title: skip and limit for pagination
exercises.instructions:
Given 10 products, implement `getPage(List<Product> all, int page, int pageSize)` that returns the correct page.

Test page 2 (page size 3) of products sorted by name.

Expected output:
Page 2 (sorted by name, size 3):
Keyboard
Monitor
Mouse

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Page 2 (sorted by name, size 3):
Keyboard
Monitor
Mouse

hints: skip((page-1) * pageSize) to skip previous pages

exercises.title: Multi-step stream — filter, sort, top N
exercises.instructions:
From a sales list, get the top 3 selling products by quantity sold (only products with quantity > 100).

Sales: Mouse(350), Keyboard(85), USB Hub(420), Monitor(120), Headphones(95), Laptop Stand(280)

Expected output:
#1 USB Hub: 420 units
#2 Mouse: 350 units
#3 Laptop Stand: 280 units

test_cases.expected_stdout:
#1 USB Hub: 420 units
#2 Mouse: 350 units
#3 Laptop Stand: 280 units

hints: filter qty > 100 removes Keyboard and Headphones
Use regular for loop after collect (forEach doesn't give index)

exercises.title: Trace stream lazy evaluation + short-circuit
exercises.scenario: Streams are lazy — not everything executes.
exercises.expected_solution:
F:1
F:2
F:3
F:4
F:5
Count: 2

test_cases.expected:
F:1
F:2
F:3
F:4
F:5
Count: 2

hints: filter runs on ALL 5 elements — count() is the terminal op
count() doesn't short-circuit — needs to see all elements

exercises.title: IntStream range operations
exercises.scenario: Generate and process a range of integers.
exercises.instructions:
Use IntStream to:
1. Sum numbers 1-100
2. Find all multiples of 7 between 1-50, as a List
3. Check if all numbers 1-10 are less than 20

Expected output:
Sum 1-100: 5050
Multiples of 7 (1-50): [7, 14, 21, 28, 35, 42, 49]
All 1-10 < 20: true
hints: .boxed() converts IntStream to Stream<Integer> for collect()

exercises.title: Complete product analysis — 5 stream queries
exercises.scenario: Five stream queries on the same product list.
exercises.instructions:
Given a List<Product> (name, category, price, rating, inStock), answer 5 questions with stream pipelines:
1. Total value of in-stock inventory (sum of prices of in-stock items)
2. Names of top 2 rated products
3. Are ALL books priced under ₹1000?
4. How many distinct categories?
5. Most expensive out-of-stock item name (or 'None')

Expected output:
Inventory value: ₹9344.0
Top 2 rated: [Monitor, Keyboard]
All books under ₹1000: true
Distinct categories: 2
Most expensive OOS: Headphones

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Inventory value: ₹7794.0
Top 2 rated: [Monitor, Keyboard]
All books under ₹1000: true
Distinct categories: 2
Most expensive OOS: Headphones

hints: Inventory: filter inStock, mapToDouble price, sum()
Top 2: sort desc by rating, limit(2), map name
Books check: filter Books, allMatch price < 1000

exercises.title: Portfolio analytics — full stream pipeline
exercises.scenario: Real-world portfolio analysis using all stream operations.
exercises.instructions:
Analyze a stock portfolio. Each Holding has: symbol, shares (int), buyPrice, currentPrice.

Stream queries:
1. Total portfolio value (shares × currentPrice, sum)
2. Symbols of profitable holdings (currentPrice > buyPrice), sorted A-Z
3. Unrealized gain on top 3 holdings by gain% (currentPrice/buyPrice - 1)
4. allMatch: all holdings currently profitable?
5. Symbols as single comma-separated string, alphabetical

Expected output:
Total value: ₹281500.0
Profitable: [HDFC, RELIANCE, TCS]
Not all profitable: WIPRO at loss
Top 3 by gain%: [TCS(+41.0%), HDFC(+22.6%), RELIANCE(+11.5%)]
Portfolio: HDFC, RELIANCE, TCS, WIPRO
